title: polystichum lacerum
intro: Polystichum lacerum, also known as the broken or lacerated shield fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves that are typically 5-20 cm long and have a wavy or serrated margin. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Polystichum lacerum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to improve digestion and boost the immune system. Polystichum lacerum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
